title: Cut-sky mock test — does the DESI topological anomaly survive survey geometry?
authority: JCAP simulated referee report (R3-1, R2-4, R1-4); internal code audit phase6_bgs_tda.py + phase6_bgs_voxelize.py
motivation: Code audit confirmed the canonical DESI TDA runs gudhi CubicalComplex on the FULL 128^3 embedding cube with ~85% exterior voxels set to 0.0; the mask is used only for thresholds/mean, NOT to exclude the exterior from the filtration. Mocks (compute_b2_full) are periodic boxes with no mask and mode='wrap' smoothing. The two statistics are therefore not like-for-like. This gate tests whether the anomaly is an artefact of the survey geometry / zero-exterior embedding by applying the SAME construction to the mocks.

frozen_desi_reference.note: These DESI values are UNCHANGED by this gate — the same compute_tda_features is applied. Only the mock reference distribution is rebuilt.

test_1_diagnostic.purpose: Isolate the survey-geometry effect. Answer: 'is the anomaly the mask?'
test_1_diagnostic.construction: Populate Quijote nwLH halos (groups_003, z=0.5) with B3 median HOD; assign velocities (centrals = halo bulk velocity; satellites = halo bulk velocity, NO intra-halo dispersion = option (a), matching the paper's stated approximation); tile the periodic box into the DESI NGC embedding cube; observer at Cartesian origin; convert to (RA,Dec,z_cosmo); apply RSD -> z_obs; carve by the DESI NGC mask voxel + z in [0.1,0.4]; downsample radially to match BGS n(z); voxelize via delta_FKP against the DESI random catalog with exterior set to 0.0 and smoothing R=5 (sigma_px=0.216); run compute_tda_features UNMASKED (identical to DESI canonical).
test_1_diagnostic.space: redshift-space (RSD applied; centrals must carry halo velocity or the field is real-space and reintroduces the R3-4 mismatch)
test_1_diagnostic.statistic_reporting: empirical rank / percentile of DESI within the cut-sky mock distribution. Gaussian sigma is NOT reported as primary because with N=200 the p-floor is ~1/201 (~2.6 sigma-equivalent). Any sigma quoted is auxiliary and flagged as tail extrapolation.

DISSOLVED.condition: abs(z_pers1) < 3.0 AND beta1_max_desi within [mock_mean - 3 sigma, mock_mean + 3 sigma]
DISSOLVED.meaning: The anomaly was survey geometry. Both components of the dual signature are reproduced by cut-sky LambdaCDM mocks.
DISSOLVED.action: STOP. Pivot to methodological paper (sigma_px systematic + honest nulls + 'the dual signature is the fingerprint of a truncated persistence diagram'). Do NOT proceed to N=2000.

SURVIVES.condition: z_pers1 >= 3.0 AND beta1_max_desi < mock_mean - 3 sigma (still anomalous in the same direction)
SURVIVES.meaning: Survey geometry does not explain the anomaly.
SURVIVES.action: Proceed to Test 2 (definitive masked pipeline) and scale Test 1 to N=2000. Rewrite as anomaly paper with cut-sky mocks as the reference distribution.

PARTIAL.condition: any intermediate outcome (one feature dissolves and the other does not; or significance collapses from the current 9-30 sigma to the 3-5 sigma range)
PARTIAL.meaning: Geometry explains a large part but not all of the signal.
PARTIAL.action: Downgrade to attempt. Realistic expected outcome = methodological paper with declared residual. Document exact numbers; no post-hoc threshold renegotiation.

test_2_definitive.purpose: Correct, like-for-like masked pipeline. Produces the publishable number regardless of Test 1 outcome.
test_2_definitive.construction: As Test 1 but exterior EXCLUDED from the filtration on BOTH sides (exterior field -> -inf so exterior cells enter the sublevel of -field last and generate no finite features; loops crossing the survey boundary are cut, identically for mock and DESI). Satellite velocities use option (b): halo bulk + virial dispersion sigma_v(M).
test_2_definitive.requires_desi_recompute: YES — the DESI field must be re-run through the masked (exterior-excluded) TDA to obtain a new DESI reference consistent with Test 2. Test 2 does not reuse the canonical 0.45886.
test_2_definitive.gate_2_threshold: 3.0 sigma (empirical rank at N=2000). This is the definitive publishable significance.

declared_limitations: Periodic tiling of the 1000 Mpc/h box into the 1997 Mpc/h embedding cube replicates structure at the box scale; standard for cut-sky mocks from periodic boxes, declared as a limitation.
Test 1 satellites are uniform-in-sphere (r = r_vir * u^(1/3)), not NFW (R3 minor point 2); acceptable for a diagnostic, addressed in Test 2.
Gadget group velocity convention (sqrt(a) factor at z=0.5) must be verified against Quijote documentation before the run is treated as final; controlled by a config flag.
GroupVel binary offset in the FoF reader is inferred from the documented header layout and must be validated on one real file before the full pilot.
